objective: Implement the independently verifiable F-0096 durable physical file lifecycle partition without claiming ingestion-gate closure: when an eligible draft paper_asset is deleted, atomically delete its metadata, write the existing redacted audit, and enqueue a durable local-file cleanup only when the deleted object has no remaining paper_asset reference. Process cleanup under the same object identity lock used by upload completion, recheck zero references before strict contained local deletion, and persist completed or retryable failure state. Generate only offline Drizzle migration source; do not execute migration or access a real database. Preserve published/archived retention, private download, upload idempotency and all authorization boundaries.
blockedFiles: migration execution, persistent database access or real data
download authorization, response headers or learner visibility
role, permission, entitlement or paper lifecycle expansion
dependency, Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push
F-0097, F-0098 or another independent finding

authoritativeChainEvidence.requirement: Module 02 section 3.9 requires published attachments to remain with the paper record; the validated F-0096 ledger requires durable cleanup, failure recovery and reference-safe physical lifecycle.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schema: paper_asset currently stores the content-addressed object identity but has no durable cleanup state; paper_asset_upload_operation already provides upload idempotency and must remain compatible.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: draft-only metadata delete and redacted success audit are already atomic, but delete returns only boolean and cannot durably schedule or reconcile file cleanup.
authoritativeChainEvidence.storage: strict contained private reads exist, while no strict local delete primitive or symlink-safe cleanup command exists.
authoritativeChainEvidence.serviceRuntime: the delete command can process a durable cleanup result without changing the external DTO or authorization route; upload completion must share the same object identity lock to prevent create-versus-delete races.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: schema, upload saga, delete atomicity, local storage, service/route and phase-9 runtime tests are the direct contracts and regressions.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: The usage contract commit 17de33b3c is closed. This task does not invent a paper attachment size or MIME policy and does not claim F-0096 fully closed until the separate ingestion gate is resolved from authoritative requirements.

mandatoryConcurrencyContract.lockIdentity: fixed paper_asset namespace plus exact objectKey PostgreSQL transaction advisory lock
lockParticipants: prepare upload before operation insert, recovery or existing-operation read
complete upload before operation FOR UPDATE and paper_asset insert
draft delete before paper_asset FOR UPDATE, delete, reference check and enqueue
cleanup from job FOR UPDATE recheck through local delete and outcome persistence
mandatoryConcurrencyContract.lockOrder: immutable objectKey read, advisory lock, FOR UPDATE identity and status reread
mandatoryConcurrencyContract.liveClaims: pending and file_stored upload operations defer cleanup when no paper_asset reference exists; any paper_asset reference cancels cleanup
mandatoryConcurrencyContract.identityFailure: any exact objectKey identity disagreement fails closed without file deletion
mandatoryConcurrencyContract.transactionBoundary: claim, reference and live-claim recheck, physical action and durable outcome remain in one database transaction
mandatoryConcurrencyContract.retryEntrypoint: the same DELETE command retries pending or failed cleanup by source paper_asset publicId without duplicating success audit
mandatoryConcurrencyContract.localDelete: full validated identity only; canonical containment, no symlink or directory, streaming hash and exact size; exact missing target is idempotent completion while missing storage root is retryable

validationEvidence.focused: 8 test files; 58 tests; exit 0 after formatter
validationEvidence.lint: affected schema, repository, service, runtime, storage and tests; exit 0
validationEvidence.build: Next.js production build; 100 routes; exit 0; existing NFT trace warning only
validationEvidence.formatAndDiff: targeted Prettier write completed before freeze; exact check and git diff check pending final state update
validationEvidence.schemaSource: offline Drizzle generation produced one additive cleanup enum/table/index/check migration with monotonic snapshot/journal and no migration execution
validationEvidence.adversarialReview: pass: prepare, complete, draft delete and cleanup share advisory-before-row-lock ordering; live upload claims defer, references cancel, physical delete and outcome remain in one transaction; repeated DELETE retries without duplicate success audit; local synthetic deletion rejects identity, integrity, traversal, intermediate/final symlink, directory and missing-root violations; no real database or runtime concurrency proof claimed

resumeAnchor.task: paper asset durable physical file lifecycle
resumeAnchor.condition: guardian task-bound schema/local-delete approval is bound at base 17de33b3c; proceed through TDD and conditional closeout while preserving the separate ingestion-gate residual
